questions.question: What are the 5 components of the Universal Prompt Formula?
options: Role, Task, Context, Format, Tone
Subject, Verb, Object, Adjective, Adverb
Who, What, When, Where, Why
Input, Process, Output, Review, Revise

questions.question: Why is specifying a ROLE important in your prompt?
options: It makes the prompt longer and more impressive
It influences the vocabulary, perspective, and depth of the response
It's not important, you can skip it
It only matters for creative writing tasks

questions.question: Which is an example of a SPECIFIC task (not vague)?
options: Write an email
Make a list
Write a follow-up email to a potential client who requested a demo but didn't respond after the meeting
Explain this to me

questions.question: What is 'prompt chaining'?
options: Using multiple AI tools at the same time
Breaking complex tasks into sequential prompts that build on each other
Copying the same prompt many times
Linking prompts together with special characters

questions.question: You want ChatGPT to practice a difficult conversation with you. Which technique should you use?
options: Prompt chaining
Role-playing scenarios
Using constraints
Sequential refinement

questions.question: What's the benefit of using CONSTRAINTS in your prompts?
options: They make prompts harder to write
They limit ChatGPT's capabilities
They force better, more creative, and focused output
They're only useful for poetry

questions.question: Which is a common prompt engineering MISTAKE?
options: Being too specific about what you want
Iterating and refining the output
Accepting the first output even if it's not quite right
Providing context and examples

questions.question: You get a response that's close but not perfect. What should you do?
options: Start a completely new conversation
Give up and write it yourself
Iterate with a follow-up prompt like 'This is close, but can you make it more concise?'
Use the exact same prompt again

questions.question: What does specifying FORMAT in your prompt accomplish?
options: Makes the prompt look more professional
Saves time reformatting - you get usable output immediately
It's optional and doesn't really matter
Only works for creating tables

questions.question: For a project requiring a 10-page report, what's the BEST approach?
options: Ask for the entire 10-page report in one prompt
Use prompt chaining: outline first, then expand each section sequentially
Give up because ChatGPT can't handle long content
Copy the same prompt 10 times for 10 pages
